rulings.Case Title: IN RE: NEMAN BROTHERS AND ASSOCIATES, INC., A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Homestead Exemption Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 1, 2023 line 1. LUCIA QUAN'S NOTICE OF HEARING RE: HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION DECLARATION Hearing Required to address whether judgment debtor's absence from August 31, 2022 was due to mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO PANIAGUA ET AL VS. MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 1, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is continued to March 9, 2023. On February 17, 2023 and on February 28, 2023 Plaintiff filed a request for dismissal without specifying whether the entire complaint or only some parties are dismissed. No later that March 1, 2023 by 5:00 p.m. Plaintiff to file a properly filled out request for dismissal. If the property filed request for dismissal does not moot the motion for summary adjudication, the hearing will proceed on March 9, 2023. If the dismissal is as to the moving parties on this motion, the motion will be ordered off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion By Lem Holdings, Llc And Patrick Harty For Relief From Issue And Evidence Sanctions [Ccp 473(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 1, 2023 line 7. NOTICE OF MOTION BY LEM HOLDINGS, LLC AND PATRICK HARTY FOR RELIEF FROM ISSUE AND EVIDENCE SANCTIONS [CCP 473(B)] is GRANTED. The order granting evidentiary and issue sanctions is vacated. The Motion for Issue and Evidentiary Sanctions is set for hearing on March 17, 2023. No later than March 6, 2023 Defendants to file an amended opposition including counsel declaration. The brief in support of the opposition must be substantially the same at the original brief with the exception of additions of references to the newly filed declaration. Each substantive paragraph of the declaration in support of the opposition must be referenced as evidentiary support to arguments made in the opposition brief. Any issues/facts/points raised in the newly filed declaration without any reference to the previously filed opposition will be stricken and will not be considered. All amended portions of the opposition (consisting solely of references to the declaration) shall be in bold font and underlined. No later than March 14, 2023 Plaintiff shall file a reply. No later than 9:00 a.m. on March 1, 2023 Plaintiff to submit a declaration outlining its fees and costs incurred in opposing this motion and filing the Motion for Summary Adjudication. Defendants' counsel shall pay fees and costs (determined by the Court at the 3/1/2023 hearing) to Plaintiff's counsel no later than March 6, 2023. Defendant to file a declaration confirming that payment has been timely remitted no later than March 7, 2023. Defendant to prepare a form of order in accordance with this tentative ruling, leaving the amount of costs and fees blank and present it for execution at the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SCHNEIDER VS. COCOA RESIDENTIAL OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 1, 2023 line 8. CROSS-DEFENDANT STEPHEN SCHNEIDER'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT is GRANTED. As alleged, each and every claim asserted in the cross-complaint arises out of protected activity (e.g. litigation and association management issues).
